mission.title: traefik: Traefik 3.6.0: add option to disable Traefik Hub Demo in dashboard
mission.description: Traefik 3.6.0: add option to disable Traefik Hub Demo in dashboard. Requested by 49+ users.

steps.title: Check current traefik deployment
steps.description:
Verify your traefik version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
helm list -n traefik 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working traefik installation.

steps.title: Review traefik configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant traefik configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl get configmap -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=traefik
```
### Welcome!

### What did you expect to see?

I expect to find option like:

```
--disable-dashboard-demo
```

to disable Traefik Hub Demo in dashboard.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Traefik 3.6.0: add option to disable Traefik Hub Demo in…
steps.description:
Hi there! 👋
Thanks for taking the time to share your feedback on this.
After reviewing this, we've decided to keep the demo section in the dashboard. The demo menu is collapsed by default, so it doesn't interfere with the regular workflow, and we believe it serves a valuable purpose for users who want to explore Traefik Hub's capabilities directly from their open-source installation.
We think the current implementation strikes a good balance - it's there for those who want to discover what's

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl get events -n traefik --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Traefik 3.6.0: add option to disable Traefik Hub Demo in…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Hi there! 👋
Thanks for taking the time to share your feedback on this.
After reviewing this, we've decided to keep the demo section in the dashboard. The demo menu is collapsed by default, so it doesn't interfere with the regular workflow, and we believe it serves a valuable purpose for users who want to explore Traefik Hub's capabilities directly from their open-source installation.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with traefik installed or the issue environment reproducible.
